blast_radius.weighting_notes: service.py counted 2x (core business logic); test files 0.5x; frontend component/store/hook files counted 1x each (L3 application layer). Phase 4A backend edit (service.py) is the single heavy-weight file; all Phase 4B files are frontend.
blast_radius.recommendation: Proceed — sequencing is correct (red→green→commit per task) and the overview mandates one-PR-per-phase. Splitting would violate that policy. The scope is bounded.

one_way_doors.change: ApiError widening to carry optional partial_state, failed_turn, partial_state_save_failed, partial_state_save_error (Task 1)
one_way_doors.analysis: All four fields are TypeScript optional (field?: Type). Existing consumers of ApiError are unaffected — they continue to match on error_type and status. No consumer is forced to learn about recovery state. This is not a one-way door.

The existing App.tsx already renders ConfirmDialog, SecretsPanel, CommandPalette, ShortcutsHelp as conditionally-rendered overlays at the App level (lines 246-261). RecoveryPanel follows the same established pattern. No z-index or stacking architectural debt is introduced beyond what already exists.
Zustand slice additions are additive. No existing slice fields are renamed or removed. The store interface grows; no downstream component is broken by fields it does not select.

findings.title: Plan wording preserves forbidden defensive try/except in constructor
findings.description:
The current module-tail _phase3_composer_init (service.py:4234-4237) contains:

  try:
      self._max_tool_calls_per_turn = self._settings.composer_max_tool_calls_per_turn
  except AttributeError:
      self._max_tool_calls_per_turn = 16

CLAUDE.md §'Defensive Programming: Forbidden' bans try/except to suppress attribute access errors and explicitly bans hasattr and getattr on typed dataclass fields. WebSettings.composer_max_tool_calls_per_turn is defined unconditionally at src/elspeth/web/config.py:44 (int = Field(default=16, ge=1)) and declared in the protocol at src/elspeth/web/composer/protocol.py:637. The field cannot raise AttributeError on a correctly-constructed settings object.

Task 0 Step 2's instruction reads: '_max_tool_calls_per_turn, using self._settings.composer_max_tool_calls_per_turn with the existing fallback value 16'. The phrase 'with the existing fallback value 16' is ambiguous — it could be interpreted as preserving the try/except pattern rather than acknowledging that 16 is the Pydantic default. If the implementer carries the try/except into the normal constructor body, they will have migrated forbidden defensive code into class-canonical form, which is worse than leaving it in the tail where it is at least visibly marked as a Phase 3 temporary workaround.
evidence: src/elspeth/web/composer/service.py:4234-4237 — current try/except AttributeError pattern
src/elspeth/web/config.py:44 — composer_max_tool_calls_per_turn: int = Field(default=16, ge=1)
src/elspeth/web/composer/protocol.py:637 — protocol property declaration
CLAUDE.md §Defensive Programming: Forbidden — 'Never use .get(), getattr(), isinstance(), or silent exception handling to suppress errors from nonexistent attributes'
findings.required_fix: Rewrite the wording to: '_max_tool_calls_per_turn, assigned directly from self._settings.composer_max_tool_calls_per_turn without any try/except or fallback — the Pydantic model guarantees the field is always present with default 16 when not overridden'. The plan's Step 5 tests should then catch any regression where the field is missing from a test settings fixture.

findings.title: editorRevision counter may duplicate compositionState.version
findings.description:
Task 3 Step 2 adds editorRevision: number (or 'equivalent current edit counter') to sessionStore alongside the existing compositionState.version. The compositionState.version field (src/elspeth/web/frontend/src/types/index.ts:159) already serves as the authoritative change counter: sessionStore.ts:305-308 and 473-476 use compositionState?.version to detect version change and clear validation. The concurrent-edit guard's requirement is to detect whether the user edited the pipeline after the failed compose started. compositionState.version changes on every successful composer state update, which is precisely what 'the LLM changed the state' captures.

Adding a parallel editorRevision introduces a second counter with its own increment discipline, and the plan does not specify when editorRevision increments vs when compositionState.version increments. If they are not kept in sync, the guard can produce false positives (spurious confirmation dialogs) or false negatives (missed conflict detection).

The plan's rationale for not reusing version is not given.
evidence: src/elspeth/web/frontend/src/stores/sessionStore.ts:305-308 — existing version-change detection using compositionState.version
src/elspeth/web/frontend/src/types/index.ts:157-168 — CompositionState.version: number
Plan Task 3 Step 2 — 'recoveryStartedEditorRevision: number | null' and 'editorRevision: number or equivalent current edit counter'
findings.required_fix: Before implementation, justify why compositionState.version is not sufficient for the concurrent-edit guard. If it is sufficient, replace editorRevision with a captured compositionState.version snapshot taken at the moment the failed compose started and compare against get().compositionState?.version when Apply is triggered. Eliminate the parallel counter.

findings.title: Final gate does not re-run Phase 3 backend test suite after Phase 4A refactor
findings.description:
Task 0 Step 5 runs 6 Phase 3 backend tests to verify the structural cleanup is semantics-preserving. However, Task 9 (Final Phase 4 gate) only runs npm test, npm run typecheck, and npm run lint — all frontend. The Phase 4 PR will be opened after Task 9 completes, which means the final gate provides no backend regression signal at PR-open time. A reviewer cannot verify from the gate output alone that the Phase 4A refactor did not drift Phase 3 semantics.

This matters because Task 0 is the first commit in the branch. If subsequent tasks introduce changes that interact with service.py in ways not caught by frontend tests (e.g., a mis-merged import), the backend suite would surface it but is not re-run at gate time.
evidence: Plan Task 9 Steps 1-3 — npm test, npm run typecheck, npm run lint only
Plan Task 0 Step 5 — backend test invocation (runs only during Task 0 execution)
findings.required_fix: Add Task 9 Step 0 or insert a backend sub-step: re-run the Task 0 Step 5 command before opening the PR. The same 6-test invocation from Task 0 Step 5 is sufficient.

findings.title: Appendix count fix piggybacked into structural refactor task
findings.description:
Task 0 Step 4 modifies docs/superpowers/plans/completed/composer-progress-persistence/2026-04-30-composer-progress-persistence-phase-3-compose-loop.md to correct an appendix count (38 → 39). This is an unrelated documentation fix piggybacked into the Phase 3 structural cleanup task. The same commit (Step 7) stages both the service.py refactor and the Phase 3 plan doc amendment.

This is minor but creates two concerns: (1) the commit message 'refactor(composer): normalize phase 3 service helpers' will not honestly describe the doc amendment, so a reviewer skimming the message will not know the Phase 3 plan document changed; (2) if the Phase 3 manifest count requires re-verification, it should be a standalone doc-only commit with a --no-verify annotation per the project convention for markdown-only changes.
evidence: Plan Task 0 Step 4 — doc count fix
Plan Task 0 Step 7 — single commit for both service.py refactor and plan doc
MEMORY.md: 'Doc-only commits don't need CI hooks — markdown-only commits → git commit --no-verify directly'
findings.required_fix: Either split Step 4 into a separate doc-only commit before the refactor commit, or add the doc filename to the commit message summary so reviewers are not surprised. Preferred: separate doc-only commit at the start of Task 0, then the service.py refactor commit at Step 7 without the doc file staged.

findings.title: Phase 4A and Phase 4B share a PR despite different risk profiles
findings.description:
The overview states 'each phase is one PR'. Phase 4A (backend structural cleanup) and Phase 4B (frontend feature) are merged into one PR by this plan. This is a deliberate policy decision and not a plan defect per se, but it creates a risk: if Phase 4B frontend work surfaces a problem requiring a rollback, the rollback also reverts the Phase 4A cleanup. Phase 4A is a pure structural improvement with no behaviour change; ideally it would be independently mergeable. The plan does mitigate this by explicitly stating Phase 4A 'must not change HTTP response shape, persistence semantics, audit/logging semantics, database schema, or public composer tool behaviour', and the test guard in Task 0 Step 1 provides machine-enforced proof that the monkeypatching is gone.

This is flagged as a warning because the one-PR-per-phase policy is the operator's architectural choice. However, if Phase 4B is blocked or needs significant rework, the team should be prepared to land Phase 4A alone by opening an interim PR targeting RC5.2 before the full Phase 4 PR.
evidence: Overview: 'Four sequential phases. Each phase is one PR.'
Plan header: 'Phase 4A is a backend-structure cleanup only... Phase 4B is frontend/client recovery'
findings.required_fix: No code change required. Document in the PR description that Phase 4A and Phase 4B can be reviewed as independent units if needed, and that Phase 4A can be extracted and merged separately without re-review if Phase 4B is delayed.

findings.title: Null handling for assistant_message_id and transcript_url is correct trust-boundary behaviour
findings.description: Task 5 Step 1 covers 'handles failed_turn.assistant_message_id === null' with a 'documented degraded diagnostic instead of throwing'. CLAUDE.md §Data Manifesto confirms that absent fields from external/API responses are Tier 3 data. The backend explicitly returns failed_turn.transcript_url = null today (plan baseline). Rendering a degraded diagnostic instead of throwing is the correct pattern for Tier 3 absence — it records 'we got null' rather than fabricating a URL or crashing the panel. This is not fabrication and is not a defensive programming violation.
evidence: Plan baseline: 'src/elspeth/web/sessions/routes.py::_failed_turn_response_body() emits transcript_url: None'
CLAUDE.md §Tier 3 External Data: 'Record what we didn't get — if we expected data and the external system didn't provide it, that absence is a fact worth recording'
Plan Task 1 Step 2: 'FailedTurn.transcript_url must be string | null, not string'
findings.required_fix: No change. Informational confirmation that null-handling in Task 5 is architecturally sound.

findings.title: fetchRecoveryTranscript limit:500 is a deliberate documented constraint
findings.description: The plan hardcodes limit: 500 with no cursor in fetchRecoveryTranscript and explicitly states 'Do not add since= unless the backend endpoint has been changed in an earlier PR. If a future backend adds a cursor, update this helper and its tests in the same PR.' The backend endpoint's current parameter support (include_tool_rows=true, limit, offset — no since) is validated against live code in the plan baseline. This is not backloaded scope; it is an explicit boundary matching the current API contract with a documented upgrade path.
evidence: Plan baseline: 'GET /api/sessions/{sid}/messages accepts include_tool_rows=true, limit, offset, include_llm_audit, and include_raw_content; it does not accept since'
Plan Task 1 Step 3 note on cursor deferral
findings.required_fix: No change. Informational confirmation that the 500-row hard limit is a deliberate scope decision, not an oversight.

findings.title: Phase 4A backend changes remain within L3 application layer
findings.description: service.py lives in src/elspeth/web/composer/ (L3 application layer per CLAUDE.md §Layer Dependency Rules). Task 0's refactor moves module-tail functions into the class body; no new cross-layer imports are introduced. The monkeypatched helper functions (_phase3_run_one_turn_for_test, _phase3_serialize_response_via_walker, _phase3_state_payload_for_compose_turn_for_test) already import from elspeth.web.composer.redaction (L3) and elspeth.core.canonical (L1 — downward). Moving them into the class changes nothing about the import graph.
evidence: service.py:4169-4170 — existing imports in the helpers: from elspeth.core.canonical import canonical_json; from elspeth.web.composer.redaction import ...
service.py:4197-4198 — from elspeth.web.sessions._persist_payload import StatePayload (L3→L3 sibling)
CLAUDE.md §Layer Dependency Rules — L3 can import L0, L1, L2
findings.required_fix: No change. Informational confirmation that layer rules are satisfied.

pattern_alignment.plan_approach: Task 0 Step 2 wording is ambiguous — 'with the existing fallback value 16' may preserve try/except
RecoveryPanel rendered at App level using useFocusTrap + local dialog markup
pattern_alignment.project_standard: SecretsPanel (inline style fixed), CatalogDrawer (useFocusTrap), ConfirmDialog (useFocusTrap + alertdialog role)

pattern_alignment.plan_approach: recoveryError stored in sessionStore slice, opened from sendMessage/retryMessage catch paths
pattern_alignment.project_standard: Existing error field in sessionStore, error dispatch in catch blocks at lines 337-376, 502-526

pattern_alignment.plan_approach: Each task writes failing tests first, then implements, then commits
pattern_alignment.project_standard: Overview ground rules: 'TDD throughout. Each phase's tasks follow Red → Green → Refactor → Commit'

pattern_alignment.plan_approach: transcript_url typed as string|null; null renders degraded diagnostic
pattern_alignment.project_standard: Tier 3 handling — record absence as null, let consumer decide

sequencing_assessment.notes: Task ordering is logically sound: Phase 4A cleanup (Task 0) precedes all Phase 4B frontend work. Tasks 1→3→4→5→6→7 are correctly sequenced: types before clients, clients before store, store before components, components before App rendering. Task 8 (manual smoke) after Task 7 is correct. Task 9 gate comes last. The single gap is W-02: Task 9 does not re-run the Phase 3 backend suite.

tracer_bullet.rationale: Phase 4B does not have more than 8 sequential steps before integration. Task 7 wires the component into App.tsx and verifies end-to-end in Task 8. The phases are already structured as incremental slices landing in commit sequence.

summary.architectural_concerns: The only architectural concern requiring action before implementation is F-01: the plan's Task 0 Step 2 wording is ambiguous and may cause the implementer to carry the forbidden defensive try/except AttributeError pattern from the monkeypatch tail into the clean constructor body. This must be resolved before Task 0 is executed.
summary.overall_structure: The plan is architecturally sound. Phase 4A's structural cleanup is well-scoped, properly test-gated, and Layer-compliant. Phase 4B's frontend feature follows existing patterns (store slice, App-level overlay, useFocusTrap). The ApiError widening is non-breaking. The one-way-door analysis finds no irreversible changes without mitigation.
